_comment: AGENTS.md §12 (drift watchlist) as enforced code — architecture-hardening.md R1. Every PERMITTED same-logic-in->=2-places duplicate in this repo is enumerated here with its copies (file + a regex that must still match there) and its lock (the fixture/generated-file/test that catches drift, or 'unlocked' when none exists yet). scripts/drift-check.mjs (just check-drift / just check) enforces: (a) every copy exists where claimed, (b) every declared lock file exists and lockStatus is consistent with whether locks are registered, (c) for entries with a 'scan' block, no NEW file outside the registered copies matches the concept's detection pattern. Registration friction is the feature — do not add entries for concepts that haven't actually drifted or burned us; do not delete an entry because its pattern is inconvenient, tighten the pattern instead.

entries.description: Per-keystroke note rules (title/tag/preview/wikilink/image) — the ONE sanctioned TS mirror of the Rust note domain (AGENTS.md §4 M6/M7). Copies must stay bit-for-bit per tests/conformance/*.json.
Sync server URL validation, hand-written x3 (no Rust side). TS, Swift, and Kotlin all read the full shared fixture.
entries.note: LOCKED: All three consumers are fully locked against server-url.json; Kotlin's SyncManagerDefaultsTest.kt loads and asserts all 9 fixture cases.

entries.description: Note sort order (modified desc, id asc). The Rust store is canonical: snapshots are emitted sorted and every mutation carries per-note sorted positions (issue #35 / ADR-0001), so the three former shell copies are deleted — shells apply engine positions as verbatim splices. The ONLY remaining twin is the browser in-memory dev/test harness, the LocalNoteStore seam's second engine adapter. Each side is pinned by its own contract tests (crates/futo-notes-store tests.rs; src/lib/localNoteStore.test.ts); no shared fixture locks the pair.

Dev/prod notes-root split (M3 CRITICAL): 3 independent copies by design. AGENTS.md §6 explicitly accepts this as a non-goal for unification — registered so it's visible, not to be locked or collapsed.
entries.note: Deliberately accepted duplication (M3 CRITICAL guard) per AGENTS.md §6 non-goals — do not attempt to unify or lock this; it is registered so the drift registry documents ALL permitted duplicates, including the ones that stay prose-only. CORRECTION to AGENTS.md §12: the Android copy is NOT in NotesStore.kt (that class only takes a resolved `notesRoot: File`) — the actual dev/prod guard lives in NotesStorage.kt's `deviceFolderName`, and Android's split works differently (a distinct DEVICE-mode folder name, 'FUTO Notes Dev' vs 'FUTO Notes', since its APP/INTERNAL modes already isolate via the `.dev` applicationId on package-scoped paths) rather than the desktop/iOS 'fake-notes' vs 'futo-notes' directory-name split.

entries.description: 5s filesystem-watcher self-write suppression window: Rust const + TS write-suppression TTL literals. Locked via PKT-7's constants.json gate (F21).
Safe note-ID validation at the TypeScript platform boundary and Rust note vault. Both consumers read tests/conformance/path-safety.json so acceptance cannot silently diverge.

The '-2, -3, ...' unique-id suffix resolver. Rust is canonical for desktop and native. The only TS copy is the browser-only development/test note-store harness. Separate tests protect each implementation; no shared fixture currently locks them.

MAX_TITLE_LENGTH (200) and the visible forbidden-character set, shared by packages/editor/src/filename.ts and Rust, and generated into both native shells as a live-input-filter fast path (apps/ios/Sources/TitleSpec.swift, apps/android/.../TitleSpec.kt) via scripts/gen-title-spec.ts. The numeric 200 is additionally locked cross-language via constants.json (TS+Rust only). Platform-specific control-character handling remains template-authored: Android matches the TS C0 + DEL set, while iOS matches Rust's wider Unicode control-character rule.
entries.note: PARTIAL: generation locks MAX_TITLE_LENGTH and the visible forbidden characters from packages/editor/src/filename.ts into both native files. Control-character handling is still authored in the generator templates: Android/TS use C0 + DEL while iOS/Rust use the wider Unicode control set, so the whole rule is not fully conformance-locked.

entries.description: Dated conflict-copy naming ("<base> (conflict YYYY-MM-DD)", counter on a same-day collision, never stacking on an existing conflict suffix). Rust rule in futo-notes-core conflict_names (used by sync parks and the note engine's flush_draft park arm); the browser LocalNoteStore harness plays the engine side of the seam and mirrors the dated-token subset (it never mints sync's object-id tokens). Each side's flush contract test pins the exact minted names. A THIRD, hand-rolled copy still lives in Android's NoteEditorScreen.kt (SimpleDateFormat mint, no counter, no suffix-peeling — and it has already drifted: a device-LOCAL date vs the registered pair's UTC). It is UNLOCKED and slated for deletion in #38, when Android adopts the flush_draft verb; registered here so check-drift is honest until then. Partial: the Rust/TS pair has independent test pins, Android has none.
